decision: Fit to run for chapters 17 through 50 after the fixes in this review. The scripts now refuse uncertain inputs and failed work instead of publishing a plausible artifact or reporting success.

faults.fault: The model reply was written directly to brief-N.json without parsing it. Empty output, prose, malformed JSON, and fenced JSON could all become a misleading brief artifact.
faults.fix: Accept raw or wholly fenced JSON, reject prose/malformed/empty replies, validate the exact brief shape, and atomically write only a validated brief.

faults.fault: A brief carried no machine-checkable chapter identity, so a plausible brief for the wrong chapter could be handed to the printer under the requested filename.
faults.fix: Briefs now carry chapter and title fields copied from CONTENTS.md; prep validates them before writing and print validates them before launching.

faults.fault: A missing OpenRouter key became an Authorization header containing None and failed later at the network seam, obscuring the real problem.
faults.fix: Validate the credential structure and non-empty key before the request without printing or persisting the key.

faults.fault: Writing a brief directly to its final path could truncate or replace a good brief before a failed write completed.
faults.fix: Write to a repository-local temporary file and replace the destination atomically only after serialization succeeds.

faults.fault: The filename map stopped at chapter 27, so chapters 28 through 50 failed later with a dictionary lookup even though tonight's run covers them.
faults.fix: Build a complete 17-50 catalog from the closed CONTENTS.md, require exactly one entry for every number, derive deterministic filenames from each verified title, and preserve the established abbreviated chapter 19 filename.

faults.fault: The title regular expression had no completeness proof. A missed or wrongly captured CONTENTS.md entry was only discovered at lookup or after a page had been launched with that title.
faults.fix: The catalog parser rejects duplicate, empty, or missing entries and the --catalog mode exposes every derived title and filename for inspection. All 34 rows were checked against CONTENTS.md.

faults.fault: The printer stripped possible code fences from a brief but never parsed or validated the JSON, so prose or a brief with the wrong fields could still be sent to the writer as authoritative input.
faults.fix: Parse raw or wholly fenced JSON and enforce the same schema, non-empty must_cover list, chapter, and title as prep.

faults.fault: The script printed codex-exec's return code but always exited successfully itself, including when codex-exec failed.
faults.fix: A non-zero runner return now terminates the printer with a non-zero result.

faults.fault: A zero runner return was accepted without proving the requested page was created or changed, carried the exact chapter heading, or passed the required mechanical checks.
faults.fix: Require changed page bytes, the exact CONTENTS-derived first line, readable head-chef JSON, and every named head-chef requirement before reporting success.

faults.fault: A missing chapter glob was silently skipped and multiple matches silently selected the first file, so the reader could confidently report on a thinner or arbitrary batch.
faults.fix: Require exactly one regular book/NN-*.md match for every requested chapter and verify that page's heading identifies the same chapter.

faults.fault: Non-canonical, duplicate, or out-of-order chapter arguments could produce misleading chapter labels and output filenames.
faults.fix: Accept only unique, increasing canonical chapter numbers from 17 through 50.

faults.fault: When the reader model failed, stderr was written as though it were the missing-material report and the script still exited successfully; an empty successful reply was also published.
faults.fix: Publish only non-empty stdout from a zero-return model run, write it atomically, and otherwise fail without replacing a report.

title_filename_table.title: Checking it before it leaves the kitchen
When one waiter is not enough
When one cook is not enough
Two people working at once, without colliding
When the menu grows from ten dishes to a thousand
When the day is longer than one shift
Knowing which restaurant you are standing in
Changing the manual everywhere at once
Two kitchens, same recipe, different dish
A worker never hires a worker
What we deliberately left out, and why

verification.result: passed: chapter 16, 470 body words, required structure present, no jargon hits, no label suspects
passed: emitted 34 entries, chapters 17-50; every title and filename above checked by eye against CONTENTS.md

verification.command: mocked prep response and reader glob gate tests
verification.result: passed: fenced JSON accepted; prose, wrong identity, invalid batches, and ambiguous globs rejected; 170-* did not match chapter 17

verification.command: python3 press/read-for-missing.py 19 while chapter 19 is absent
verification.result: passed refusal test: exited non-zero and named the exact missing book/19-*.md match
passed refusal test: chapter 50 resolved correctly, then exited non-zero before launching because its required brief was absent
